title: Meesho Data Scientist - I
content:
# Meesho Data Scientist - I

Tier 2 College

Previously did internship (SWE) at a global finance company

Base: 24LPA
CTC: 60LPA
Yearly Bonus: 
7.5 Year 1
8.5 Year 2
9.5 Year 3
10.5 Year 4

In round 2, questions where on ML fundamentals and projects and designing a recsys for a problem and explaining the components.

Overall, it was medium-level of toughness. Work is same as applied scientist @ Amazon with may be some minor differences

title: How Do Eric Emanuel Shorts Bring Luxury Into Everyday Fashion?
content: # Why Are Eric Emanuel Shorts Different From Regular Mesh Shorts? Modern fashion lovers are always searching for premium streetwear, and ee shorts continue leading that movement. What makes eric emanuel shorts unique is their ability to combine basketball-inspired fashion with luxury comfort. Unlike ordinary mesh shorts, eric emanuel shorts feature standout colors, premium stitching, and a designer fit. The popularity of ee shorts keeps rising because they fit perfectly into urban fashion culture. The vision of eric emanuel has turned these shorts into more than just clothing—they are a lifestyle statement. Fashion enthusiasts wear eric emanuel shorts with oversized tops, premium sneakers, and accessories for a complete streetwear look. That is why ee shorts continue gaining attention from fashion lovers around the world. # Why Is Eric Emanuel Dominating Premium Streetwear? The success of eric emanuel comes from understanding what fashion lovers want. People want comfort, authenticity, and modern design, and eric emanuel shorts deliver all three. Whether for summer outfits, travel, or casual styling, ee shorts provide comfort and style without compromise. eric emanuel e shorts, ee shorts, and eric emanuel collections continue becoming essential pieces in luxury streetwear culture.

title: NetApp | MTS 3 | Offer Evaluation | Bangalore
content:
Education: Btech CSE
Years of Experience: 7.5
Prior Experience: Product based company
Previous CTC: 30 LPA

Offer:
Company: NetApp
Position: MTS 3
Base: 41LPA (including employers PF)
Stocks: $22000 over 3 years
Bonus: 2.5LPA (8%)
Total CTC: ~ 43.5 LPA + Stocks (~6.5 LPA)
Total CTC with Stocks: ~ 50 LPA

Is this a good offer? How is the work culture at NetApp?

**Work-Life Balance:** How is it at NetApp?
**Learning & Growth Opportunities:** Does the company provide good exposure to new technologies and growth in career paths?
**Job Stability:** Can anyone share their perspective on job security and the stability of teams?
**Variable/Bonus Payout:** They also have bonus (8%) in the CTC, can someone from NetApp comment how much % of Bonus is usually given on an average ?
**Yearly Appraisals:** On an average how much % hikes are given yearly ?

Any inputs or personal experiences would be greatly appreciated as I evaluate this opportunity. Also, are there any other pros and cons worth noting ?

Thanks in Advance!

title: US MNC - aSWE Interview Experience
content:
Education: B.Tech in CSE, Tier 3 College
Years of Experience: Fresher
Level: Associate Software Engineer (aSWE)
CTC: 9,00,000 INR LPA
Interview Mode: Walk-In

==============================================

# Screening Round: Aptitude Test

Format: Pen and paper test.

Experience: It was relatively easy but acted as a massive filter. It thinned the crowd from around 150+ candidates down to roughly 60-70.

# Round 1: Technical Face-to-Face

Format: 1-on-1 with a Senior SWE.

### Questions:

DSA: Started with Valid Parentheses (explained the Stack approach). Then moved to Next Greater Element, which I coded and passed all test cases for.

Core Java / Frameworks: Asked a few standard questions on Spring Boot to test my backend knowledge.

Database: Asked me to write an SQL query to find the 2nd highest salary. I wrote a solution handling duplicate salaries using DISTINCT and OFFSET. The interviewer then asked me to explain the exact difference between OFFSET and LIMIT on paper, which I did.

Vibe: Very straightforward technical round. Once I finished the queries, I asked him about their backend stack, and we had a good chat.

About 30 candidates passed this round.

# Round 2: Architecture & Logic

Format: 1-on-1 with a VP.

### Questions:

Behavioral: Asked about my family background and why my previous 6-month internship didn’t convert into a full-time offer (explained industry headcount/budget issues).

DSA (Live Coding): Jumped onto an online compiler. I had to write executable code with custom inputs for Valid Parentheses (again) and Daily Temperatures (Next Warmer Day). Both executed perfectly.

Java Fundamentals: Deep dive into some of the tricky Java questions from the Aptitude sheet (e.g., how == differs from .equals() under the hood).

Puzzle: Given a 3L bucket and a 5L bucket, how do you measure exactly 4L of water? I mapped out the pouring steps on paper to show exactly how to isolate 4L in the bigger bucket.

About 10 candidates passed this round.

# Round 3: Panel Round

Format: Zoom Panel with another VP and a Senior Staff member.

### Questions:

Tech Journey: Asked why I chose Java for backend development when I originally had a Python background. I explained how my internship put me on a Java Migration team, which showed me how reliable enterprise-grade Java really is.

Project Deep-Dive: Asked about the flow and components of my backend projects. Asked about the limitations of my system. I explained how I was running a local 3B parameter LLM and discussed alternatives for scaling trading agents.

Research & System Design: Asked why I published a research paper instead of a full app (explained API cost limitations at the time). Then asked: "If you were to build an API for a client, what is your exit criteria?" I explained my process: gathering requirements, writing exhaustive test cases for edge cases, and proactively communicating with the client if there is any ambiguity before handing off the final API.

DevOps: Quick discussion on Kubernetes deployment strategies based on my resume.

Vibe: Very conversational. They wanted to see my product mindset and how I handle client/team handoffs.

About 5-7 candidates recieved the offer.

# Final Thoughts
Patience is key: Walk-in drives test your physical endurance as much as your coding skills. Bring water and be ready to wait hours between rounds.

Know your projects: The panel rounds focused heavily on why I made certain architectural decisions and how I would handle production edge cases.

Communicate your logic: For puzzles and SQL queries, writing your thoughts on paper and explaining them out loud to the interviewer is just as important as getting the right answer.

**Best** of luck to everyone still grinding. The market is tough right now, but consistency pays off!

title: Nielsen MTS 2 || Compensation || Bangalore
content:
***Education:*** Tier 1 CSE, 2023 Passout
***Work exp:*** SDE1 at US Based well known MNC (laid off)
Prev Comp: 
22 (fixed) , No refresher stocks [Promotion was blocked due to layoffs]

***Nielsen Comp:*** 30 base (fixed) + 0 variable
**Other Benefits**
- 5 lakh standard medical insurance
- 3k/month for food as food is not free in office
- Free office cabs
- 5 days WFO (min 6 hours)
- notice period: 15 days for first 6 months (probation period) , 60 days afterwards

Sent dm to recruiter on Linkedin in response to hiring post. Received response almost 2-3 weeks later. Then scheduled all Interviews in quick succession.

Accepted the offer as I dont have any other competing offers rn and 30 was their max budget. Feedback for all Interviews was strong positive so they gave their max.
Also Interviews in other comps going too slow (ignorant recruiters). Dont want a career break.
How is wlb, culture and appraisals in Nielsen?

**CP Stats**: 
~1850 Rated on cf
~2450 Rated on lc
~2150 Rated on cc

***Prepartion***: 
For DSA - Practised leetcode top 200 Qs alongwith top company recent Qs by tag on lc.
For LLD -  started with Shreyansh Jain , then watched InterviewWithBunny YT and finally did mock rounds with ChatGPT.
For HLD - started with Shreyansh Jain, then shifted to Hello Interview then watched InterviewWithBunny and finally did mock rounds with ChatGPT.
For HM - Did mock with Chatgpt alongwith Resume Grinding.

Interview experience - https://leetcode.com/discuss/post/8215196/nielsen-mts2-interview-experience-bangal-q2t8/

title: Got Support Engineer Offer - Amazon
content:
Hi everyone,

I recently received an offer from Amazon for a Support Engineer role.

**Compensation details:**
Base Pay: ₹8,23,000
Sign-on Bonus Year 1: ₹2,96,000
Sign-on Bonus Year 2: ₹2,66,000
RSU Value over 4 years: ₹7,23,000

I am currently in my final year of B.Tech from a Tier 3 college and will be joining as a fresher.

To be honest, my goal was always to get an SDE role, so I am feeling a little disappointed. I feel I am capable of doing more, but given my current situation and the opportunities I have, I don’t really have another strong option right now. So, most likely, I will have to join this role.

I wanted to get honest feedback from people who have been in a similar situation. Is it possible to grow from a Support Engineer role and later transition into an SDE role, either within Amazon or outside? What should I focus on after joining so that I don’t get stuck in support and can move towards development?

Any advice from people working at Amazon, in support engineering roles, or those who transitioned from support to SDE would really help.

title: Google L4/L3 SWE - ML in GPU performance
content:
Aug 2025 - Recruiter reached out and completed application
Sep 2025 - GHA completed, scheduling first round of interviews
Oct 2025 - Completed first round and proceed to next round of interviews
Nov 2025 - Final Interviews completed, recruiter conveyed that results are trending towards L4
End of Nov 2025 - team matching - 2 teams. Proceeds with 1 HM and then recruiter goes ahead for HRC. Later I get rejected and reason is not specified. On 6 month cooldown. 
Jan 2026 - Sourcer reaches out again for a potential opportunity. 
Feb 2026 - New recruiter assigned, this time I get to speak with HM who asked to reinitiate process for me. Convo goes well
Mar 2026 - 2 additional GPU programming and technical interviews scheduled and completed.
Apr 2026 - Passed interviews, got a team match done with other HM instead of previous HM due to headcount issues. Spoke with new HM and they agree to move forward. Also a call with one senior team member was setup which went well.
May 2026 - recruiter mentions that HRC was completed with offer for L3. 

Given that my previous interviews from last year and this year did signal trending L4, is there a way I can ask them to up-level position? I have 3 years of experience, currently in semiconductor based company. 

**Current Compensation
Base : 190k, bonus: 40k, equity: 60k/yr (currently as per value of stock)**

Does google give such competitive offer for L3? According to what I saw on levels.fyi, I see this range is mostly for L4/L5, any tips for negotiating would be highly appreciated. Thanks in advance!

#google #googleinterview #compensation
